author: Dr. Lizard
title: How does ICC define myeloid neoplasm with mutated TP53 differently from WHO-5?
answer: ICC requires TP53 VAF at least 10% and recognizes multi-hit equivalent with complex karyotype.
detail: ICC multi-hit criteria include two mutations each VAF 10% or one mutation VAF 10% plus deletion 17p.
Complex karyotype plus one TP53 mutation VAF 10% qualifies as multi-hit equivalent in ICC only.
ICC can diagnose mutated TP53 myeloid neoplasm without requiring dysplasia if multi-hit criteria are met.
ICC creates separate categories for 10-19% blasts as MDS/AML and 20% or more as AML with mutated TP53.
releaseNote: ICC defines myeloid neoplasm with mutated TP53 differently from WHO-5 by requiring TP53 VAF at least 10% and recognizing multi-hit equivalent with complex karyotype. ICC also creates separate categories for 10-19% blasts as MDS/AML and 20% or more as AML with mutated TP53.
